title: My trading bot, actually works, actualy does make something
author: onlyoffence
selftext:
Been working on this bot for 3 motnhs now. Actually trades crypto and analyzes bets with proper chances straight from polymarket. 

1. Extremely cheap, less than 10cents per day  
2. Very sophisticated (6-7/10 wins per day)  
3. Runs on mobile and browser  
4. Runs 24/7

At the moment i am showing only specific parts of the bot -there are more screens that reveal more details and strategies running in the background, but i am not going to show those. 

This is not a for sale product, its only for personal use and will always be. 

Just wanted to share this as i am sure more people are willing to build one. My point is..dont listen to negativity, try, test, rework and it might work. 

Thats all.

comments.author: Dependent_Stay_6954
comments.body:
This looks cool on the surface, but there’s not enough here to tell if it’s actually profitable in a statistically meaningful way.
A few key things missing:
“6–7 wins per day” doesn’t mean anything without loss size and frequency. You can have a high win rate and still lose money if losses are larger than wins.
No starting balance or % return shown — a $70k account doesn’t tell us anything about performance.
No equity curve, drawdown, or trade distribution. That’s what actually proves a system works.
The P&L per trade looks small, which suggests a scalping/micro-edge strategy — these usually break once you factor in spread, slippage, and scaling.
“<10 cents per day” is likely just compute cost, not actual trading friction (which is the real killer in live systems).
Also, running 24/7 is an advantage, but only if the strategy holds across all market regimes — most don’t without proper validation.
Not saying it doesn’t work — just that there’s no real evidence here yet.
To make this credible, you’d need:
Full trade log (large sample size)
Profit factor
Max drawdown
Equity curve over time
Results including fees/slippage
Until then, this is just a snapshot, not proof of edge.

comments.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
comments.body:
Hey there, I have my own profitable ML-based trading model for BTC (and it also works on Gold). I’ve tested it on about 5 years of data and it averaged roughly around 10% per month with fixed lot. It has a sharpe ratio of >3. I don’t have much capital myself, so I’m looking for genuine people who might be interested to buying it. If anyone’s genuinely interested, feel free to message me.

This is my report - [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1xwNisxVslkfPPY9g2rcbWt4shrI-9o4-/view?usp=drive\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1xwNisxVslkfPPY9g2rcbWt4shrI-9o4-/view?usp=drive_link)

Also to say I am live trading it since September and inspite of Spot Drawdown my model has actually generated positive returns.

comments.author: Forsaken_Leader_8
comments.body: using polymarket as a source for probability is a big brain move. most people just stare at rsi but prediction markets are usually way faster at pricing in real-world events. i’ve been building a similar research layer and found that cross-referencing prediction data with institutional flow is where the real edge is. i started using signalwhisper to pull in congressional trades and dark pool data to see if the 'smart money' aligns with the polymarket odds. it’s saved me from a few 'false' signals where the betting volume was just retail hype. mad respect for the 24/7 mobile setup, that's not easy to optimize

replies.author: onlyoffence
replies.body: Thanks, i have so much more to share about the 24/7 on poylmarket, I will be posting more soon

comments.author: FilmFreak1082
comments.body:
Respect for building it yourself and keeping it personal. The "stop calling it a scam" edit tells me you got the same reaction I did when I first posted about mine lol. People see "trading bot" and immediately assume the worst.

The Polymarket intelligence layer is interesting - are you using prediction market data to influence your crypto trading decisions? Like if theres a high probability event on Polymarket that would impact crypto prices, does the bot factor that into entries? Thats a creative edge if so.

6-7 wins out of 10 on futures/leveraged trades is solid. I trade spot only so my win rate is higher (92%+) but the gains per trade are smaller since theres no leverage. Different tradeoff.

I went the opposite direction from you - built mine specifically to share with others. Android app, connects to Binance via API, spot only. Took over a year to get the multi-user security right (zero-knowledge encryption so the server never sees API keys in plaintext). Hardest part wasnt the algo, it was making it simple enough that someone with zero trading knowledge can use it.

Keep building. The people calling it a scam have never built anything.

replies.author: onlyoffence
replies.body: I will be sharing my polymarket strategy soon in another post. Unfrotuantely, people calling scam anything and everything nowadays -even if u dont ask for money or something

replies.body: Yeah the scam accusations come with the territory unfortunately. The moment you say "trading bot" people assume the worst. Just keep posting real results and let the data speak. Looking forward to seeing the polymarket strategy breakdown - thats the part that genuinely interests me.

comments.author: flowprompt-ai
comments.body: The architecture here is what stands out. Combining a crypto trading bot with a prediction market intelligence layer is a multi-model problem and the fact that you built it for personal use rather than to sell is what makes the results more credible than most things posted in this thread. The Polymarket edge as a signal input is a genuinely novel approach. This kind of composable intelligence design is exactly what our Crypto Investment AI hackathon track is built around. [flowprompt.ai](http://flowprompt.ai) if you ever want to build the next version with more infrastructure behind it.
